name: Home - Community Action Partnership of Hennepin County
description:
Building a Hennepin County Without Poverty.

Community Action Partnership of Hennepin County works with our community to provide services that meet immediate needs and build financial independence.

How Can We Support You?

We’ve designed programs and services to help meet basic needs so you can not just survive, but thrive.

Energy Assistance

You might spend as much as 25% of your income on utility bills. Get help with your gas and electric bills through our Energy Assistance Program.

Vehicle Repair

With our Vehicle Repair Program, you can get help paying for vehicle repairs so you can have safe and reliable transportation to work.

Rental Assistance

We provide a one-time payment for eligible households so you can get caught up on rent or get help toward a security deposit.

Water Assistance

Behind on your water bills? Community Action Partnership of Hennepin County can help you pay down your balance.

Tax Filing Resources

Need help filing your taxes? Free and low-cost tax filing resources are available.

MNsure Application Assistance

Not sure how to complete your MNsure application? We can help you apply so you can access low or no-cost coverage.

Partners in Fighting Poverty

You can accept the things you can’t change, or you can change the things you can’t accept.

And with 1 in 10 Hennepin County residents living below the Federal Poverty Line, we’re working with partners like you to change the story and fight poverty in our communities.

WHO WE ARE
Workshops and Counseling for You
Employment Readiness

Our Employment Readiness Program helps residents with low income find jobs that pay a living wage.

Financial Wellness

We provide free workshops and counseling to give you the skills to make a budget, manage your money, and reach your financial goals.

Homebuyer Services

Learn all about homeownership and how to make a plan that’s right for you through Homebuyer Workshops and free Homebuyer Counseling.

Disrupting the Cycle of Poverty

Community Action Partnership of Hennepin County is committed to fighting poverty and the factors that cause it. With comprehensive programs and dedicated staff, we create pathways for people to move from poverty to prosperity.

SERVICES PROVIDED JAN.-DEC. 2025
Energy Services & Water Assistance

19,616

Housing Services
Financial Wellness Services
Employment Services, MNsure Application, and Vehicle Repair Assistance
Get Involved
DONATE
VOLUNTEER
SHARE YOUR STORY

name: Energy Assistance - Community Action Partnership of Hennepin County
description:
2025-2026 Energy Assistance Program

Community Action Partnership of Hennepin County’s (CAP-HC’s) Energy Assistance Program can help pay your utility bills between October 1, 2025 and May 31, 2026. If you’re an eligible applicant, you may receive a grant paid directly to your utility provider that reduces your expenses.

We process applications in the order they are received, except for families who have no heat and need urgent help. Currently, staff are working on applications as they are received.

ELIGIBILITY
CRISIS ASSISTANCE
GET STARTED
WEATHERIZATION REFERRALS
Note: The Cold-Weather Rule starts October 1 and ends April 30. Contact your utility provider for more information.
Eligibility

You can determine if you’re eligible by looking at your gross income, which is your income before any taxes and deductions.

Have a household of 9 or more people? Call 952-930-3541 for income eligibility information.

You can find additional eligibility and documentation requirements in the Hennepin County Energy Assistance application.

GET STARTED

INCOME ELIGIBILITY REQUIREMENTS

HOUSEHOLD SIZE MAXIMUM MONTHLY GROSS INCOME*
1 $3,119
2 $4,079
3 $5,039
4 $5,999
5 $6,959
6 $7,919
7 $8,099
8 $8,279
*Gross income — total earnings before taxes and other deductions
Crisis Assistance

Your household may be eligible to receive up to $600 in crisis funds if:

You have an active utility disconnection.
Your propane or other delivered fuel is under 20% or currently shut off.
You are 60+ years old with a current or past-due bill.
IF YOU’VE ALREADY SUBMITTED AN ENERGY ASSISTANCE APPLICATION THIS SEASON
IF YOU HAVEN’T SUBMITTED AN ENERGY ASSISTANCE APPLICATION THIS SEASON
Get Started

To get Energy Assistance, you need to complete an application and submit all required documentation to determine if you’re eligible.

Ready to apply for Energy Assistance? Here’s how to get started:

FILL OUT AN APPLICATION
COLLECT REQUIRED DOCUMENTS
SUBMIT YOUR APPLICATION
How to Submit Your Application

You can submit your completed application and additional documentation in one of the following ways:

Online:

If you applied using the Department of Commerce’s online application, your application materials are automatically submitted for review.

Mail:

CAP-HC Energy Assistance Program
1101 East 78th Street, Suite 110
Bloomington, MN 55420

Email:

eap@caphennepin.org

Fax:

952-697-1313

In person:

Visit one of CAP-HC’s offices during office hours to drop off your application and documentation.

Your application materials will be reviewed, and you will be notified of next steps through the contact information you provided.
 
Still have questions? Please call us at 952-930-3541.

Weatherization Referrals

With the Weatherization Assistance Program, you may be eligible for free energy upgrades for the home you rent or own, which could save energy and make sure your home is a safe and healthy place to live.

Services may include:

Installing insulation
Reducing air leaks
Performing heatin

name: Energy-Related Repair - Community Action Partnership of Hennepin County
description:
Energy-Related Repair Program

When your heating system works well, it decreases energy use, lowers utility bills, and keeps your home safe and warm through the coldest months of the year.

If you’re eligible for the Energy-Related Repair Program from Community Action Partnership of Hennepin County (CAP-HC), you can get repair or replacement for heating systems that aren’t working.

Need immediate assistance with your heating system? Please call 952-930-3541 at any time — 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.

We process applications in the order they are received, except for families who have no heat and need urgent help. Currently, staff are working on applications as they are received.

ELIGIBILITY
GET STARTED
Eligibility

You’re eligible for assistance with your heating system if you meet the following requirements:

You live in Hennepin County.
You’re a homeowner.
You’re eligible for the Energy Assistance program, which is determined by gross income and other requirements outlined in the application available below.

Note: The Energy-Related Repair Program does not repair or replace water heaters or central air units.

Have a household of 9 or more people? Call 952-930-3541 for income eligibility information.

INCOME ELIGIBILITY REQUIREMENTS

HOUSEHOLD SIZE MAXIMUM MONTHLY GROSS INCOME*
1 $3,119
2 $4,079
3 $5,039
4 $5,999
5 $6,959
6 $7,919
7 $8,099
8 $8,279
*Gross income — total earnings before taxes and other deductions
Get Started

The Energy-Related Repair program is part of the Energy Assistance Program and runs from October 1 to May 31 each year.

To get started, complete an Energy Assistance application and submit all required documentation. You can complete your application in one of the following ways:

FILL OUT AN APPLICATION
COLLECT REQUIRED DOCUMENTS
SUBMIT YOUR APPLICATION
How to Submit Your Application

You can submit your completed application and additional documentation in one of the following ways:

Online:

If you applied using the Department of Commerce’s online application, your application materials are automatically submitted for review.

Mail:

CAP-HC Energy Assistance Program
1101 East 78th Street, Suite 110
Bloomington, MN 55420

Email:

eap@caphennepin.org

Fax:

952-697-1313

In person:

Visit one of CAP-HC’s offices during office hours to drop off your application and documentation.

Your application materials will be reviewed, and you will be notified of next steps through the contact information you provided.
 
Still have questions? Please call us at 952-930-3541.

Collect Required Documents
Provide proof for the following:
Wages: EAP may use your SSN to verify wages reported by your employer. We may ask you to provide check stubs or other verification if we are unable to verify your wages. If your income has recently gone down you will need to provide proof of your most recent full calendar month of wages from the month before you sign your application.
MFIP, GA, DWP: County statement showing monthly amount or bank st

name: Hennepin County Rental Assistance Programs
description:
Rental Assistance

Community Action Partnership of Hennepin County’s (CAP-HC’s) Rental Assistance program can help pay your past-due rent or security deposit. This assistance can ensure you and your family have stable housing, even during a financial crisis.

We process applications in the order they are received. Right now, staff are working on applications sent in the week of:

DECEMBER 15, 2025

ELIGIBILITY
GET STARTED
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
Eligibility

You’re eligible for Rental Assistance if you meet the following requirements:

You live in Hennepin County.
You do not receive any rental subsidy or housing voucher, like MPHA or Section 8.
Your rent is past due, and you are still living in the unit you are requesting Rental Assistance for.
You have household income that is at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level Income Guidelines — see table.
If seeking security deposit assistance:
You have a denial letter from Hennepin County Emergency Assistance or other rental assistance provider.

Please note: 

Applicants may only apply for CAP-HC’s Rental Assistance program once every 24 months.
Applicants must complete CAP-HC’s Financial Wellness or Renter’s Rights Workshop as part of the application process.

Have a household of 9 or more people? Call 952-933-9639 for income eligibility information.

INCOME ELIGIBILITY REQUIREMENTS

HOUSEHOLD SIZE MAXIMUM MONTHLY GROSS INCOME* MAXIMUM ANNUAL GROSS INCOME*
1 $2,660.00 $31,920
2 $3,606.67 $43,280
3 $4,553.33 $54,640
4 $5,500.00 $66,000
5 $6,446.67 $77,360
6 $7,393.33 $88,720
7 $8,340.00 $100,080
8 $9,286.67 $111,440
*Gross income — total earnings before taxes and other deductions
Get Started

To get Rental Assistance, you need to complete an application and submit all required documentation to determine if you’re eligible.

Ready to apply for Rental Assistance? Here’s how to get started:

FILL OUT AN APPLICATION
COLLECT REQUIRED DOCUMENTS
SUBMIT YOUR APPLICATION
How to Submit Your Application

You can submit your completed application and additional documentation in one of the following ways:

Mail:

CAP-HC Rental Assistance Program
7101 Northland Circle N, Suite 123
Brooklyn Park, MN 55428

Email:

rentalassistance@caphennepin.org

In person:

Visit one of CAP-HC’s offices to drop off your application and documentation.

We will review your application materials and follow up with you for next steps.

Please note:

We process applications in the order that we receive them. Due to high demand, it may take up to 60 days to process your application.
Rental Assistance clients must complete CAP-HC’s Financial Wellness or Renter’s Rights training and submit their completion certificate before assistance grants will be paid to property managers.

Still have questions or want to have an application mailed to you? Please email us at rentalassistance@caphennepin.org.

Frequently Asked Questions
WHAT ARE THE QUALIFICATIONS FOR RENTAL ASSISTANCE?
CAN I APPLY FOR RENTAL ASSISTANCE IF I RECEIVE SECTION 8 S

name: Hennepin County Vehicle Repair Assistance Programs
description:
Vehicle Repair Assistance

Community Action Partnership of Hennepin County’s (CAP-HC’s) Vehicle Repair Assistance Program can help you repair your car so that you can maintain a safe and reliable way to get to work.

ELIGIBILITY
GET STARTED
Eligibility

You’re eligible for CAP-HC’s Vehicle Repair Assistance Program if you meet the following requirements:

You live in Hennepin County.
You have a valid Minnesota driver’s license.
You have current car insurance.
You have proof of ownership of the vehicle in need of repair.
Your car is less than 20 years old.
You have household income that is at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level Income Guidelines — see table.

Please note:
Applicants must complete CAP-HC’s Financial Wellness Workshop as part of the application process.

Have a household of 9 or more people? Call 952-933-9639 for income eligibility information.

INCOME ELIGIBILITY REQUIREMENTS

HOUSEHOLD SIZE MAXIMUM MONTHLY GROSS INCOME* MAXIMUM ANNUAL GROSS INCOME*
1 $2,660.00 $31,920
2 $3,606.67 $43,280
3 $4,553.33 $54,640
4 $5,500.00 $66,000
5 $6,446.67 $77,360
6 $7,393.33 $88,720
7 $8,340.00 $100,080
8 $9,286.67 $111,440
*Gross income — total earnings before taxes and other deductions
Get Started

To get Vehicle Repair Assistance, you need to complete an application and submit all required documentation to determine if you’re eligible.

Ready to apply for Vehicle Repair Assistance? Here’s how to get started:

FILL OUT AN APPLICATION
COLLECT REQUIRED DOCUMENTS
SUBMIT YOUR APPLICATION
How to Submit Your Application

You can submit your completed application and additional documentation in one of the following ways:

Mail:

CAP-HC Vehicle Repair Program
7101 Northland Circle N, Suite 123
Brooklyn Park, MN 55428

Email:

vehiclerepair@caphennepin.org

In person:

Visit one of CAP-HC’s offices to drop off your application and documentation.

We will review your application materials and follow up with you for next steps.

Please note:
All Vehicle Repair Program clients must complete CAP-HC’s Financial Wellness training and submit the completion certificate before vehicle repairs are started.

Still have questions or want to have an application mailed to you? Please email us at vehiclerepair@caphennepin.org.

name: MNSure Application Assistance in Hennepin County
description:
MNsure Application Assistance

Whether you’re uninsured, underinsured, or just have questions about health insurance, Community Action Partnership of Hennepin County’s (CAP-HC’s) Navigators provide in-person MNsure application assistance so you can access low or no-cost coverage for you and any members of your household.

ELIGIBILITY
GET STARTED
PREPARE FOR YOUR APPOINTMENT
Eligibility

You’re eligible for CAP-HC’s MNsure Application Assistance if you meet the following requirements:

You live in Hennepin County.
You are uninsured or underinsured.

Members of a federally recognized American Indian tribe can also sign up through any Community Action organization at any time throughout the year.

Get Started

Please note: CAP-HC’s MNsure Application Assistance program does not process MNsure applications, and a separate CAP-HC application is required.

Ready to complete your CAP-HC application for MNsure Application Assistance? Here’s how to get started:

FILL OUT A CAP-HC APPLICATION
SUBMIT YOUR CAP-HC APPLICATION
How to Submit Your CAP-HC Application

You can submit your completed application in one of the following ways:

Mail:

CAP-HC MNsure Application Assistance
7101 Northland Circle N, Suite 123
Brooklyn Park, MN 55428

Email:

CAPHCMNsure@caphennepin.org

In person:

Visit one of CAP-HC’s offices to drop off your application.

We will review your application materials and follow up with you for next steps. If you’re eligible, we will contact you to make an appointment.

Still have questions or want to have an application mailed to you? Please email us at CAPHCMNsure@caphennepin.org. You may also reach out to Director of Client Services, Angelique Flemons, at aflemons@caphennepin.org or Executive Director, Dr. Clarence Hightower, at chightower@caphennepin.org.

Looking for Medicare information? Please visit the Blue Cross’ Medicare page to learn about Medicare plans available in your area.

Prepare for Your Appointment

Bring the following documentation to your appointment:

Social Security number for each person applying
Date of birth for everyone in your household
Driver’s license, Tribal ID, or another form of identification
Your tax forms from the last year you filed (Form 1040EZ or first page of Form 1040)
Your two most recent pay stubs
Documents for other income (if you have any)
Information about any employer-provided health insurance available to each person applying (you may need to request this from your employer)
If you have an existing MNsure account, bring your username and password
If you are a U.S. non-citizen, bring a green card or other immigration documents

name: Renter Workshops & Counseling - Community Action Partnership of Hennepin County
description:
Renter Workshops & Counseling

Community Action Partnership of Hennepin County (CAP-HC) provides workshops and counseling so you have everything you need to know to rent with success.

RENTER WORKSHOPS
RENTER COUNSELING
Renter Workshops

These FREE workshops will answer your questions regarding your rights and responsibilities as a renter, how fair housing laws protect you, how affordable housing is calculated, the importance of insurance, and more.

CAP-HC’s Renter’s Rights Workshops are free and open to the public.

Advanced registration is required.

Still have questions? Email us at renterrights@caphennepin.org or call us at 952-933-9639.

RENTER WORKSHOP EVENTS
Renter Counseling

CAP-HC can help you understand everything you need to know as a renter. A counselor can help you:

Review your financial situation to determine a healthy budget for rent and help you identify a good fit for you and your household
Understand programs that can assist you financially as well laws that are in place to protect renters everywhere
Plan for success when you rent, including guidance on deposits and leases and managing the relationship with your landlord

Renter Counseling is open to all Hennepin County residents. Schedule your counseling session today!

REQUEST RENTER COUNSELING
Request Renter Counseling

Community Action Partnership of Hennepin County has counselors available to help you navigate the rental process.

Counselors can help you:

Develop household budgets, determine how much rent you can afford to pay, and identify an affordable place to rent.
Establish whether you are eligible for assistance and help you apply if eligible.
Navigate the rental process, from paying deposits to signing or ending a lease.
Build and keep a good relationship with your landlord and help resolve landlord-tenant disputes.
Learn about financial planning and good rental practices, such as obtaining rental insurance and maintaining a healthy home.

Schedule your counseling session today!

Eligibility

Renter Counseling is open to all Hennepin County residents.

Contact Information

name: Homebuyer Workshops & Counseling - Community Action Partnership of Hennepin County
description:
Homebuyer Workshops & Counseling

Community Action Partnership of Hennepin County (CAP-HC) provides workshops and counseling to prepare you for every step on your path to homeownership.

HOMEBUYER WORKSHOPS
HOMEBUYER COUNSELING
PROGRAM STANDARDS
Homebuyer Workshops

Our Home Stretch Workshops can help you determine if you’re ready to own a home and make a plan to get there. Home Stretch Workshops were developed by the Minnesota Homeownership Center and explore every step of the homebuying process, including the mortgage process, shopping for a home, and overcoming barriers from credit debt, income, and savings.

CAP-HC’s Home Stretch Workshops are open to the public. Advanced registration is required, and the $40 workshop fee can be waived for income-eligible households.

Still have questions? Email us at homebuyereducation@caphennepin.org or call us at 952-933-9639.

HOMEBUYER WORKSHOP EVENTS
Homebuyer Counseling

Dreaming of owning your own home? HUD-certified counselors from CAP-HC can help you understand the process from start to finish. A counselor can help you: 

Review your credit and finances so you know how close you are to homeownership and how to make it the rest of the way
Understand the homebuyer process, including the mortgage process and programs that can make it easier to buy a home sooner
Create a plan to achieve your dream of owning your own home

Homebuyer Counseling is open to all Hennepin County residents. Schedule your counseling session with a HUD-certified counselor today!

REQUEST HOMEBUYER COUNSELING
Program Standards

Home Stretch meets or exceeds the standards for the following agencies and programs:

U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
Neighborhood Stabilization Program (NSP)
Minnesota Housing
LIFT Program
Federal Housing Administration
Fannie Mae
Freddie MAC
Request Homebuyer Counseling

Dream of owning your own home but don’t know where to start? Community Action Partnership of Hennepin County’s (CAP-HC’s) homeownership counseling can help!

HUD*-certified Housing Counselors work with clients to determine homebuyer readiness by reviewing finances, checking your credit report, and creating a budget. Then counselors help clients develop an action plan to achieve their homeownership goals. Whether you are thinking about purchasing a home some day or have already found your dream home, our Housing Counselors are available to answer your questions.

A counselor can help you with the following mortgage readiness steps:

Review your finances
Review credit (FREE credit report)
Make a credit improvement plan if needed
Explain the mortgage process
Determine the amount of mortgage you may be eligible for
Identify mortgage loans and down payment assistance programs you may be eligible for.
Make referrals to lenders approved for 1st-time homebuyer programs

Schedule your counseling session with a HUD*-certified Housing Counselor today!

Eligibility

Homebuyer Counseling is open to all Hennepin County residents.

*U.
